name: Safinamide
description: Safinamide is for the treatment of parkinson's disease. It was approved in Europe in February 2015, and in the United States on March 21, 2017.
indication: Safinamide is indicated as an add-on treatment to levodopa with or without other medicines for Parkinson’s disease
toxicity: uncontrolled involuntary movement, falls, nausea, and trouble sleeping or falling asleep (insomnia) Expected overdose effects are hypertension (high blood pressure), orthostatic hypotension, hallucinations, psychomotor agitation, nausea, vomiting, and dyskinesia.
mechanismOfAction: Safinamide is a unique molecule with multiple mechanisms of action and a very high therapeutic index. It combines potent, selective, and reversible inhibition of MAO-B with blockade of voltage-dependent Na+ and Ca2+ channels and inhibition of glutamate release. Safinamide has neuroprotective and neurorescuing effects in MPTP-treated mice, in the rat kainic acid, and in the gerbil ischemia model.

interactions.name: Dapoxetine
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Dapoxetine is combined with Safinamide.

interactions.name: Fusidic Acid
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Safinamide can be increased when it is combined with Fusidic Acid.

interactions.name: Conivaptan
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Conivaptan can be increased when it is combined with Safinamide.

interactions.name: Mitotane
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Safinamide can be decreased when it is combined with Mitotane.

interactions.name: Vemurafenib
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Safinamide can be decreased when it is combined with Vemurafenib.

interactions.name: L-Tryptophan
interactions.description: L-Tryptophan may increase the serotonergic activities of Safinamide.

interactions.name: Stiripentol
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Safinamide can be increased when it is combined with Stiripentol.

interactions.name: Nevirapine
interactions.description: The metabolism of Safinamide can be increased when combined with Nevirapine.
